methods.description: Process for preparing and measuring radionuclides in marine sediment samples to achieve secular balance and accurate measurement using a p-HPGe detector.

steps.description: Condition between 180 g to 380 g of dry sample in polypropylene containers of 300 cm3 volume, hermetically seal, weigh with a 1 mg precision balance, and store for a month to achieve secular balance between 226 Ra and 222 Rn.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and store dry samples in sealed containers for secular balance

steps.description: Perform measurements using a p-HPGe detector with a relative efficiency of 70%, resolution of 1.9 keV for the 1332.5 keV peak of 60 Co, and a peak/Compton ratio of 70.
Use Genie 2K version 2.0 program for acquisition and spectral treatment, correcting for natural background and spectral interference of 214 Bi at the 661.65 keV peak of 137 Cs.
steps.searchable_summary: Process spectral data using Genie 2K software

steps.description: Calibrate the measurement system in energy using a standard source of 152 Eu and in absolute efficiency using a known mass quantity.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate measurement system with standard source

steps.description: Establish a distance of 18 mm between the sample and the detector for optimal counting statistics, considering possible coincidence effects in gamma peaks.
steps.searchable_summary: Set measurement geometry for optimal statistics

steps.description: Correct gamma attenuation effect using the transmittance method with a calibrated source of 152 Eu.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply transmittance method for gamma attenuation correction

steps.description: Calculate the activity concentration of radionuclides in marine sediment samples using a specific equation.
